name: National Memorial Cemetery of the Pacific
tagline: A sacred resting place inside an extinct volcanic crater — free to visit, with sweeping views of Honolulu and a humbling sense of history.
parking: Free parking inside the crater. Drive up Puowaina Drive
bestTime: Early morning for cooler temps and fewer visitors. Combine with a Tantalus Drive loop
talkStory: Known locally as Punchbowl, this cemetery sits inside the Puowaina crater — 'Puowaina' means 'Hill of Sacrifice' in Hawaiian. Over 53,000 veterans are interred here, including those from WWI, WWII, Korea, and Vietnam. The Courts of the Missing list 28,000+ names of service members whose remains were never recovered. The views of Honolulu from the crater rim are stunning. This is not a tourist attraction — it's a place of deep respect. Keep voices low, stay on paths, and be mindful. Free admission, no reservations needed.
